name: Text Classification Pipeline
overview: End-to-end pipeline from raw text to deployed classifier, covering EDA, preprocessing, feature extraction, model selection, training, evaluation, and export.

steps.name: Data Loading and EDA
steps.what: Load raw text data and inspect class distribution, text lengths, and data quality.
steps.decision: Check class balance with value_counts; if the largest class exceeds 60% of the data, apply stratified sampling or class weighting before training.

steps.name: Preprocessing
steps.what: Clean and normalize text by lowercasing, removing punctuation, and tokenizing.
steps.decision: Lowercase for TF-IDF baselines to reduce vocabulary size. For BERT fine-tuning, preserve original casing because the tokenizer is cased-aware.

steps.name: Feature Extraction
steps.what: Convert text into numerical features suitable for machine learning.
steps.decision: Use TF-IDF for datasets under 50K samples with clear keyword signals. Use dense embeddings for datasets above 50K or where semantic similarity matters more than exact keyword matching.

steps.name: Model Selection
steps.what: Choose a classification model that matches the feature representation and dataset size.
steps.decision: LogisticRegression with TF-IDF as a fast baseline. Switch to BERT fine-tuning when the baseline F1 is below 0.85 and the dataset has at least 1000 labeled examples per class.

steps.name: Training and Evaluation
steps.what: Fit the model and evaluate on a held-out test set.
steps.decision: Always use a stratified train-test split with stratify=y to preserve class ratios. Report macro-F1 for imbalanced datasets and weighted-F1 for balanced datasets.

steps.name: Export
steps.what: Serialize the trained model and pipeline for deployment.
steps.decision: Use joblib for sklearn Pipelines to preserve the entire vectorizer-to-classifier flow. Use save_pretrained for HuggingFace models to export both tokenizer and weights.

common_failure_points: Class imbalance where the majority class dominates accuracy but macro-F1 collapses, giving a false impression of model quality.
Label leakage from including metadata in the text that correlates with the target but will not be available at inference time.
Tokenization mismatch between TF-IDF vectorizer and BERT tokenizer, causing the same word to be split differently and producing incompatible feature spaces.
Overfitting on small datasets when using BERT without early stopping or data augmentation, resulting in high training accuracy but poor generalization.
Evaluation data leakage through preprocessing steps like stopword removal or TF-IDF fitting on the entire dataset before splitting, which silently biases the test set.
